trade.summary: Bilateral trade has collapsed. Taiwan imposed comprehensive sanctions on Russia following the 2022 Ukraine invasion, including semiconductor export controls — highly significant given Taiwan's dominance in chip production. Pre-2022 Taiwan exported semiconductors and electronics to Russia and imported Russian raw materials. All semiconductor and advanced technology exports are banned, and Taiwan has aligned fully with Western sanctions. This represents one of the most damaging trade restrictions for Russia's military-industrial complex.
disputes: Taiwan's semiconductor export ban is a major Russian grievance — cutting off critical chip supplies
Russia criticized Taiwan's sanctions as politically motivated

diplomatic.status: hostile — mutual antagonism, Taiwan supports Ukraine, Russia supports China's Taiwan claims
diplomatic.summary: Russia-Taiwan relations are deeply adversarial. Russia supports China's position that Taiwan is an inseparable part of China, denies Taiwan's de facto sovereignty, and has actively backed Beijing's pressure campaigns against Taiwan internationally. Taiwan, conversely, views Russian aggression in Ukraine as directly analogous to potential Chinese aggression against Taiwan and has been among the most vocal non-official voices supporting Ukraine and implementing anti-Russia measures. Taiwan's sanctions on Russia, particularly the semiconductor export ban, are among the most strategically damaging any actor has imposed.
recent_incidents: Taiwan imposed comprehensive sanctions on Russia (2022)
Taiwan's semiconductor export ban directly impacted Russian military production
Russia continues to assert Taiwan is part of China
Taiwan drew explicit parallels between Ukraine and Taiwan scenarios

military.summary: No bilateral military relationship — Taiwan and Russia have no diplomatic relations. Russia's provision of military technology to China (which threatens Taiwan) creates an indirect military-strategic adversarial relationship. Russia has publicly supported China's right to use force to 'reunify' Taiwan. Taiwan's defence establishment views Russia-China military alignment as a compounding threat.
military.alliance_status: adversarial — Russia backs China's claims over Taiwan
incidents: Russia's support for China's Taiwan position is an ongoing point of tension

financial.summary: All significant financial ties severed. Taiwanese financial institutions have aligned with Western sanctions. No Taiwanese investment in Russia; Russian assets cannot access Taiwanese financial markets. TSMC and other Taiwanese semiconductor companies comply fully with export control regulations blocking sales to Russia.

energy.summary: No bilateral energy trade. Taiwan is a major LNG importer but has committed to sourcing from non-Russian suppliers. Taiwan refused to purchase discounted Russian energy despite economic incentive, aligning with Western energy embargo.

scientific.summary: No scientific collaboration. Taiwan has no formal scientific exchange programs with Russia. Taiwanese universities have aligned with international academic isolation of Russian institutions.
